aboutHero.intro: Frontend developer with a solid background in Design. I create digital products that combine code precision with aesthetic sensibility, delivering exceptional experiences from concept to production.

about.title: About Me
about.text1: Creative Frontend Developer with over 5 years of experience in Design, now focused on software engineering. Specialized in React, TypeScript, and modern frontend architectures.
about.text2: My journey combines creative vision with technical thinking, allowing me to develop interfaces that not only visually delight but are also performant, accessible, and scalable.
about.text3: Experience building complete products: from Design Systems and Figma prototyping to implementation with React, Supabase integration, and production deployment. Focus on clean code, componentization, and best practices.
about.quote: I believe exceptional code goes beyond functionality—it must be elegant, maintainable, and create experiences that delight users.

items.quote: Paula built our analytics dashboard from scratch with React and TypeScript. The code is clean, well-documented, and the performance is exceptional. Her attention to UI details made all the difference.
items.author: Rafael Mendes

items.quote: We hired Paula to refactor our legacy frontend. She implemented a consistent Design System, componentized the entire application, and reduced our bundle by 40%. Complete professional.

items.author: André Ferreira

website.challenge: The client needed a comprehensive digital platform that went far beyond a simple institutional website. The Product Design challenge involved mapping the journey of a user seeking family counseling (often in an emotionally vulnerable state), designing a flow that establishes immediate trust and comfort. From a Frontend Engineering perspective, the requirement was an architecture capable of deeply integrating active lead generation and 100% automated scheduling, with blazing-fast performance to maximize technical SEO indexing without relying on slow, rigid third-party systems.
website.solution: As a Product Designer, I mapped the user journey via flowcharts and drafted wireframes focused on accessibility (WCAG) and low cognitive friction, defining a Design System that reflected credibility through typography and color palette. In implementation, I assumed the Frontend Engineering role by building the application from scratch using React and TypeScript. I structured a highly reusable component ecosystem and developed custom asynchronous hooks to handle the scheduling and lead capture APIs. The result is a robust, lightning-fast platform highly optimized for conversion.

process.title: Discovery
process.desc: Competitor analysis and persona definition focused on the target audience (families seeking consulting).

process.title: Wireframing
process.desc: Information architecture and user flows iteratively created in Figma.

process.title: Development
process.desc: React implementation with strict focus on Core Web Vitals and scalable architecture.

process.title: Optimization
process.desc: Fine-tuning of Technical SEO and loading performance.

results: Simplified journey with a 3-step conversion flow
Technical performance with LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) < 1.2s

portfolio.challenge: Create a portfolio that wasn't just a static gallery, but living proof of my technical skills. The goal was to build a complete React application demonstrating a solid grasp of global state, complex animations, and strict typing.
portfolio.solution: I developed this website from scratch using React and TypeScript. The interface uses Tailwind for efficient styling and Framer Motion to orchestrate entrance animations and micro-interactions, creating a fluid and modern experience without compromising performance.

process.desc: Definition of the 'Industrial Noir' aesthetic.

process.title: Architecture
process.desc: Setup of the React + TypeScript + Vite project.

process.title: Motion Design
process.desc: Implementation of animations using Framer Motion.

process.title: Internationalization
process.desc: i18n configuration for 3 languages.

microsaas.challenge: Traditional distance learning suffers from high dropout rates and low engagement. Conventional platforms fail to keep long-term student interest, especially in specific cultural niches like Umbanda studies.
microsaas.solution: I developed an LMS (Learning Management System) focused on gamification. The platform uses level progression, achievements, and visual rewards to keep the student engaged. The architecture supports video streaming and interactive quizzes in real time.

process.title: Research
process.desc: Study on engagement in e-learning.

process.title: Prototyping
process.desc: Design of the gamification system.

process.title: MVP
process.desc: Development of core functionalities.

process.title: Testing
process.desc: Validation with a beta student group.

results: Instant feedback through a reactive interface

dashboard.challenge: Healthcare content creators face metric dispersion across multiple platforms. The core UX and Data Visualization challenge was to build a visual hierarchy that delivered engagement insights in a 3-second read, focusing entirely on a 'mobile-first' layout. Technologically, rendering complex charts in a mobile browser with data from multiple social networks demanded maximum DOM optimization and asynchronous components.
dashboard.solution: I created an iterative design subsystem focused on high contrast and DataViz legibility, refining each analytical component in Figma prior to coding. In development, I employed advanced libraries like Recharts in conjunction with React and TypeScript. State isolation and 'lazy loading' techniques ensured that numerical mapping occurred without delay. Instead of simply plotting charts, the interface cross-references engagement data versus lead capture, explicitly showing the user which format is the most profitable.

process.title: Information Architecture
process.desc: Reorganization of creators' analytical priorities (e.g. conversions vs empty views).

process.title: Mobile-First UX
process.desc: Creation of dense charts and simplified tab navigation for small screens.

process.title: Recharts Integration
process.desc: Rendering of canvas-based visualizations optimized for low repaint latency.

process.title: Memory Testing
process.desc: Strict garbage collection via React Profiler, avoiding memory leaks on charts.

results: Creation of complex DataViz components for Mobile
Optimized rendering avoiding memory leaks in charts
Architectural evolution of data visualization in healthcare

platform.challenge: Beauty professionals lose up to 10 hours a week on manual management. WhatsApp scheduling, paper contracts, and lack of financial control hinder business growth. The challenge was to create an 'All-in-One' platform that was powerful, yet featured a minimalist and luxurious aesthetic ('Industrial Noir') to attract this demanding audience.
platform.solution: I developed KONTROL, a complete ecosystem. It uses React + Vite for instantaneous performance and Supabase for a scalable serverless backend. The 'Industrial Noir' interface isn't just styling; it's a minimal customized Design System focused on reducing cognitive load. Features include automatic PDF contract generation, a drag-and-drop calendar, and real-time financial dashboards.

process.title: Conception
process.desc: Identification of pain points within the luxury beauty market.

process.title: Design System
process.desc: Creation of the 'Industrial Noir' visual language in Figma.

process.title: Development (Frontend & Backend)
process.desc: Implementation using React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and a real-time Supabase database.

process.title: Scalability & Payments
process.desc: Creation of the PDF Contracts architecture and Stripe integration.

results: 10+ hours saved per week per user
